ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect.
Examination: Teeth 15, 44, and 46 are missing, with well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized gingiva, and good alveolar ridge form without significant depression. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint function. Teeth 26, 34, and 36 have sutures in place with well-healed wounds.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 15 and 46 show adequate bone volume and quality. Tooth 44 shows adequate bone quality but insufficient volume.
Diagnosis: Teeth 15, 26, 34, 36, 44, and 46: dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prostheses for teeth 15, 44, and 46. Suture removal for teeth 34 and 36.
Management: For teeth 15, 44, and 46, informed consent was obtained, and the patient signed the consent form for implant restoration. After routine disinfection, local infiltration anesthesia with 4% articaine was administered. Circular incisions were made at the crest of the alveolar ridge for tooth 15, and horizontal incisions for teeth 44 and 46. Full-thickness flaps were elevated, and osteotomies were prepared to the planned depth. A BEGO implant (3.75x10mm) was placed in tooth 15 with a healing abutment (4.5x5mm), and BEGO implants (3.75x10mm and 4.5x10mm) were placed in teeth 44 and 46 with cover screws. Tension-free suturing was performed, and radiographic verification was satisfactory. Sutures were removed from teeth 34 and 36 after disinfection.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if any discomfort occurs. Avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours. Begin eating 2 hours post-procedure, avoiding hot foods.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Orthodontic extraction.
Examination: Teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45 show proper crown eruption with visible ink-stained discoloration on occlusal surfaces, rough texture upon probing, negative percussion, and no discomfort on thermal tests.
Radiographic Examination: CBCT shows complete root development for teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Diagnosis: Teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45 identified for orthodontic extraction.
Treatment Plan: Extraction of teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Treatment Recommendations: Extraction of teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Management: For teeth 24 and 35, after povidone-iodine disinfection, 1.7ml of 4% Articaine with epinephrine was administered for infiltration anesthesia, gingival separation was performed, teeth were luxated and extracted, and hemostasis was achieved with cotton ball pressure.
Medical Instructions: Follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151) with pigmentation. Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling and air polishing.
Examination: Generalized gingival swelling with easy bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets detected. Presence of calculus and pigmentation.
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151) with pigmentation.
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling and air polishing.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis and consented to treatment. Plaque disclosing, full-mouth ultrasonic scaling, air polishing, fluoride varnish application, and iodine glycerin were performed. Oral hygiene instruction (OHI) was provided, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with mouthwash. Regular check-ups and follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Single arch edentulism (K08.101). Maxillary arch edentulism. Treatment: Maxillary ALL-ON-6 implant-supported prosthesis.
Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26 are missing. The gingiva at the edentulous sites is healthy, with good alveolar ridge form and adequate gingival-occlusal distance. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement. No other abnormalities noted.
Radiographic Examination: Adequate bone volume and quality observed for teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26.
Diagnosis: Single arch edentulism (K08.101). Maxillary arch edentulism.
Treatment Plan: Placement of ALL-ON-6 implant-supported prosthesis for teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26, the patient was fully informed and consented by signing the oral implant restoration consent form. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Standard disinfection and draping were performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made along the alveolar ridge, followed by a full-thickness flap reflection. Implant sites were prepared to the planned depth, and Dentium implants were placed: 3.4*12mm for teeth 11, 21; 3.8*12mm for teeth 14, 24; 4.3*10mm for teeth 16, 26. Abutments were placed on teeth 11, 21, 14, 24, and cover screws on teeth 16, 26. Bone grafting and membrane coverage were performed. Tension-free suturing was completed, and postoperative radiographs confirmed implant positioning. The procedure lasted from 10:20 to 11:40. Impressions were taken for transitional prosthesis fabrication, and occlusion was adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: Strictly follow postoperative implant care instructions. Take oral antibiotics as prescribed. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Suture removal in two weeks.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Secondary caries. Treatment: Inlay restoration.
Examination: Tooth 36 shows a brown cavity on the distal occlusal surface with partial filling, probing to soft dentin, with an area greater than half the buccolingual width. Cold, percussion, and mobility tests are negative. No periodontal abnormalities or overeruption of opposing teeth.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Secondary caries in tooth 36.
Treatment Plan: Inlay restoration for tooth 36.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the pros and cons of different restorative materials, costs, time, and possible complications. The patient opted for a full ceramic crown. Caries removal and inlay cavity preparation were performed, followed by impression taking and shade selection.
Medical Instructions: Avoid very cold, hot, hard, or sticky foods. Return for follow-up if discomfort or loosening occurs. Schedule appointments by phone.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013). Treatment: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct intermaxillary relations, expand the dental arch, distalize molars, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, increase anterior and posterior tooth height, perform interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: Invisible aligners.
Examination: Normal overbite and overjet. Skeletal Class III with a normal mandibular plane angle. Normal anterior open bite. Normal dental arch crowding. Normal occlusal plane. Maxillary arch is ovoid, mandibular arch is square. High palatal vault. Acceptable lingual frenulum attachment. Retrusive mandible. General oral hygiene is average, with normal oral mucosa, tongue, and speech. Normal upper and lower midlines. Normal maxilla and mandible. Alveolar bone is underdeveloped in both arches. Symmetrical face with concave profile and short facial height. Normal lip-tooth relationship with full lips.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct intermaxillary relations, expand the dental arch, distalize molars, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, increase anterior and posterior tooth height, perform interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: Invisible aligners.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Photographs, oral scans, and radiographs were taken to formulate a treatment plan.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs and schedule regular check-ups.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: tooth structure defect (K07.303).
Examination: Teeth 24, 27, and 37 have temporary crowns with no soft tissue abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Tooth structure defect (K07.303) in teeth 24, 27, and 37.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 24, 27, and 37, the patient was informed of the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Temporary crowns and sealing materials were removed, abutments and restorations cleaned, and new restorations tried in for fit and contact. The fit was satisfactory, and the patient was pleased. Dual-cure resin cement was used for final cementation, occlusion was adjusted, and polishing was completed.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods. Maintain oral hygiene and follow up as needed.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: Teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 37, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47 show generalized gingival swelling and redness with easy bleeding upon probing. Presence of calculus and significant staining. Carious lesion on the occlusal surface of tooth 27.
Radiographic Examination: No significant findings.
Diagnosis: Gingivitis affecting teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 37, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47 (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling for all teeth.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Intraoral photographs were taken. Full-mouth supragingival scaling, irrigation, air polishing, and iodine glycerin application were performed. Follow-up photographs were taken for comparison and oral hygiene education was provided.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperature stimuli for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with regular use of mouthwash. Schedule regular check-ups and return if any discomfort occurs.
Remarks:
